GWAS plots

Source: vignettes/guides/gwas.Rmd
gwas.Rmd

ggpop supports Manhattan and Q-Q plots from common GWAS outputs. The intended flow is:

  1. import a typed object;
  2. plot with plot_manha() / plot_qq() or ggpop() + geom_*();
  3. use internal fastman-style Manhattan and Q-Q plotting behavior without an external fastman dependency.

API summary

Task Direct API Layer API Notes
Import GCTA/GEMMA/EMMAX import_gwas(file, type = ...) - Returns ggpop_gwas
Manhattan plot_manha() ggpop(data) + geom_manha() Defaults are aligned
Q-Q plot_qq() ggpop(data) + ggpop::geom_qq() Use explicit namespace to avoid ggplot2::geom_qq()
Backend internal fastman-style layout native ggpop stat Direct wrappers and geoms share one implementation

GCTA example 

gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta")
plot_manha(gwas)

plot_qq(gwas)

Layered workflow 

gwas |>
  ggpop() +
  geom_manha()


gwas |>
  ggpop() +
  ggpop::geom_qq()
#> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'ggpop':
#>   method                     from  
#>   print.ggpop_palette_scheme ggPopi

plot_manha(gwas) and ggpop(gwas) + geom_manha() share the same default threshold and suggestive reference lines.

GWAS colour palettes

The GWAS module uses the same unified discrete palette entry as the rest of ggpop. Manhattan plots default to a two-colour alternating chromosome palette:

plot_manha(gwas, palette = "manhattan")

To override the Manhattan colours, pass two explicit colours. binary = TRUE keeps the two-colour alternation even when a longer palette is supplied:

plot_manha(
  gwas,
  palette = c("#4E79A7", "#C4E2F3"),
  binary = TRUE
)

Reference-line colours continue to use the publication palette by default. Override them with threshold_color and suggestive_color when needed.

Alternative GWAS formats

import_gwas() accepts type = "gcta", type = "gemma", and type = "emmax". GWAS plotting uses ggpop’s internal ggplot implementation in both direct and layered workflows.

On this page

Developed by ggPopi Authors.
